top_comment.author: Mitchell_Porter
top_comment.text:
This has no direct connection to AI safety, but when I saw that the supposed goal is "to understand the true nature of the universe", I took it personally, because I've spent so much effort on precisely that goal: taught myself everything up to string theory, studied philosophers both famous and obscure, developed my own hypotheses... Maybe this is how human artists feel about the advent of AI art. 

But if I put those first feelings aside, and ask myself how this might turn out: 

One possibility is that it becomes nothing more than a summarizer of known opinions, a mix of Google Scholar, Wikipedia, and the Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy, brought to life in typical LLM fashion. 

Another is that it becomes a mouthpiece for whatever philosophy Elon Musk and his crew of AI technicians already implicitly prefer. 

Still another possibility is that a few, or even many, recognized human thinkers sign on as consultants regarding the philosophical or scientific issues that X.AI is ultimately meant to answer. 

An obvious issue is that Musk wants his AI to engage in genius-level thought, and no one knows how to automate that. You can discuss philosophy, or anything you like, with existing AIs, and the results can even be interesting, but we don't really understand the process that produces those results. (Though perhaps we have a better chance of understanding it, than of understanding human philosophical cognition.) 

Musk has also tweeted "It’s not AGI until it can solve at least one fundamental physics problem". Whether he means a solution to a quantitative problem or a...
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: Maybe this is how human artists feel about the advent of AI art.

summary: The main claim is: Some instances of optimization will not be localized, and will be spread out across the whole board.

top_comment.author: Daniel Kokotajlo
top_comment.text: I think you'd want to put more effort into making the articles more realistic. These are too short and they use fictional people. I suggest taking real articles from recent news events and modifying them to be about GPT-4/Llama/Claude/etc.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: I think you'd want to put more effort into making the articles more realistic.

summary: The main claim is: As you can see, most of the values are zeros, making the input data sparse.
In this case, the input data will be sparse, as most of the feature values will be zero.
The main claim is: If the source code is part of the OS login, the compiler will inject the backdoor from step one.

The main claim is: This means that, while an infinite chain-like state-space could record unbounded reward accumulation just like a value function, they can also record any other kind of process that cannot be modeled as reward accumulation.
The valence function can then be used for goal selection, wherein the agent chooses a policy sequence that realizes goal states which produce maximum empowerment gain.

top_comment.author: particlemania
top_comment.text: Rewards and Utilities are different concepts. To reject that reward is necessary to get/build agency is not the same thing as rejecting EU maximization as a basin of idealized agency.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: To reject that reward is necessary to get/build agency is not the same thing as rejecting EU maximization as a basin of idealized agency.
